name: Fibery MCP Server
what_it_does: Official MCP server from Fibery (Fibery-inc org) for their connected work platform. Fibery combines project management, knowledge management, and product development in a flexible, connected workspace. Enables AI agents to create and query Fibery entities, manage projects, access knowledge bases, and integrate AI into Fibery workflows.
use_cases: Creating and updating Fibery entities (tasks, features, docs) from automation agents
Querying Fibery databases and project status from reporting agents
Integrating AI workflows with Fibery's connected workspace
Building custom Fibery automations driven by AI agents
not_for: Teams using Jira, Notion, Linear, or other project tools (Fibery-specific)
Simple task management needs (Fibery is powerful but complex)
Teams not already invested in Fibery's workspace model
best_when: A team using Fibery as their primary work OS wants AI agents to interact with their Fibery workspace — querying, creating, and updating entities programmatically.
avoid_when: You use other project management tools. Fibery has a unique data model that requires familiarity — MCP tools are most useful when you understand your Fibery workspace schema.

auth.notes: Fibery API token required. Generated from Fibery workspace settings. Token scoped to user's Fibery account permissions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier for small teams (up to 5 users)
pricing.notes: Fibery has a generous free tier. MCP server is free open source from Fibery-inc. API access included in all plans.

performance.rate_limits: Fibery API rate limits apply; varies by plan

known_agent_gotchas: Fibery's dynamic schema means agents must query workspace schema before operating — types vary per workspace
Fibery uses GraphQL-style queries for entity fetching — different from typical REST patterns
Entity relationships in Fibery are rich but complex — agents may need schema introspection
Official from Fibery-inc but Fibery is a smaller company — API stability improving
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS to Fibery cloud. API token authentication. Fibery hosts workspace data in EU/US — GDPR compliant. Token is user-scoped — review permissions before agent use.

security_score_components.security_notes: API token authentication. Fibery EU/US data residency with GDPR compliance. SOC2 in progress. Token is user-permission-scoped.
